title: C# Operators
content:
### Operators in C#

Operators are symbols that perform operations on variables and values.

### Arithmetic Operators

| Operator | Name | Example | Result |
|----------|------|---------|--------|
| `+` | Addition | `5 + 3` | 8 |
| `-` | Subtraction | `5 - 3` | 2 |
| `*` | Multiplication | `5 * 3` | 15 |
| `/` | Division | `10 / 3` | 3 (int), 3.333 (double) |
| `%` | Modulus | `10 % 3` | 1 |
| `++` | Increment | `x++` or `++x` | x + 1 |
| `--` | Decrement | `x--` or `--x` | x - 1 |

### Assignment Operators

| Operator | Example | Equivalent |
|----------|---------|------------|
| `=` | `x = 5` | x = 5 |
| `+=` | `x += 3` | x = x + 3 |
| `-=` | `x -= 3` | x = x - 3 |
| `*=` | `x *= 3` | x = x * 3 |
| `/=` | `x /= 3` | x = x / 3 |
| `%=` | `x %= 3` | x = x % 3 |

### Comparison Operators

| Operator | Name | Example |
|----------|------|---------|
| `==` | Equal to | `5 == 5` true |
| `!=` | Not equal to | `5 != 3` true |
| `>` | Greater than | `5 > 3` true |
| `<` | Less than | `5 < 3` false |
| `>=` | Greater than or equal to | `5 >= 5` true |
| `<=` | Less than or equal to | `5 <= 3` false |

### Logical Operators

| Operator | Name | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `&&` | AND | True if both are true |
| `||` | OR | True if at least one is true |
| `!` | NOT | Reverses boolean value |

### Bitwise Operators

| Operator | Name | Example |
|----------|------|---------|
| `&` | Bitwise AND | `5 & 3` = 1 |
| `|` | Bitwise OR | `5 | 3` = 7 |
| `^` | Bitwise XOR | `5 ^ 3` = 6 |
| `~` | Bitwise NOT | `~5` = -6 |
| `<<` | Left shift | `5 << 1` = 10 |
| `>>` | Right shift | `5 >> 1` = 2 |

> **Key Point:** Division of two integers yields an integer (truncated). Use at least one double operand for floating-point division. Short-circuit evaluation: `&&` stops if left is false, `||` stops if left is true.

quiz.question: What is the result of 10 % 3 in C#?
Which operator is used for logical AND in C#?
